frameworks.name: collinear lateral inhibition
frameworks.one_line: Flanking Gabors reduce detectability of peripheral target via inhibitory interaction

frameworks.name: transcranial direct current stimulation (a-tDCS)
frameworks.one_line: Anodal V1 stimulation used to modulate cortical excitability

frameworks.name: perceptual learning
frameworks.one_line: Cited as the slow alternative route to reducing lateral inhibition

frameworks.name: visual crowding
frameworks.one_line: Possible behavioral consequence linked to peripheral lateral inhibition

core_question: Can a single session of anodal V1 tDCS reduce collinear lateral inhibition in normal peripheral vision?
key_assumptions: Collinear flankers at 2 wavelengths induce lateral inhibition; 6 wavelengths serves as control
Anodal tDCS over V1 modulates primary visual cortex excitability
Contrast detection thresholds index lateral inhibition strength
Sham stimulation provides an appropriate within-subject control
manipulations: Real anodal tDCS vs sham stimulation (within-subject, single-masked)
Flanker separation (2 wavelengths inhibition vs 6 wavelengths control)
Peripheral stimulus location 6 degrees from fixation
key_findings: Anodal tDCS significantly reduced detection thresholds at 2 wavelength separation
Sham stimulation produced no effect on detection thresholds
Effect was specific to the lateral inhibition condition
interpretations: Short anodal V1 tDCS can rapidly reduce collinear lateral inhibition in peripheral vision
tDCS offers a faster alternative to intensive perceptual learning for modulating peripheral suppression
